steps.title: Use the similar right triangles
steps.content: The altitude from $X$ to $YZ$ creates similar right triangles because the smaller triangle shares angle $Y$ with the original triangle. Find $\tan(\angle Y)$ from $WX$ and $WY$, then use the complementary relationship between $Y$ and $Z$.

steps.title: Find angle Y in the sub-triangle
steps.content:
Point $W$ on hypotenuse $YZ$ creates right triangle $XWY$ with a right angle at $W$ (since $WX \perp YZ$).

In this right triangle, the angle at $Y$ is the same as $\angle XYZ$ in the original triangle.

Therefore: $$\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{WX}{WY} = \frac{429}{572}$$ Both values share a common factor: $429 = 3 \times 143$ and $572 = 4 \times 143$.

So: $$\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{3 \times 143}{4 \times 143} = \frac{3}{4}$$

steps.title: Use the complementary angle relationship
steps.content: In triangle $XYZ$, angle $X = 90^{\circ}$, so the other two angles sum to $90^{\circ}$: $$\angle Y + \angle Z = 90^{\circ}$$ This means $\angle Z = 90^{\circ} - \angle Y$, so: $$\tan(\angle Z) = \tan(90^{\circ} - \angle Y) = \cot(\angle Y) = \frac{1}{\tan(\angle Y)}$$ Substituting $\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{3}{4}$: $$\tan(\angle Z) = \frac{1}{\frac{3}{4}} = \frac{4}{3}$$ That confirms D is correct.
